mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a sudden deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real GDP growth and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and HOUST both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, an increase in the industrial production index signals resilient corporate capacity utilization that supports profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the conditional forward return spread drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal savings and financial assets reflects household balance sheet strength that underpins consumer spending, mechanically supporting equity cash flows and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS changes remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical average corporate return on invested capital, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following the 5.0% threshold breach, or the forward return differential remains statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a labor market inflection point that accelerates recession risk pricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp and PAYEMS falls > 50k in the same month
failure_condition: The joint labor deterioration signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing construction activity during disinflationary periods signals improved credit conditions that boost cyclical earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to produce positive 5-day returns when CPI inflation is below 2.0%, or the conditional forward return spread drops below zero.
